zh.lifeSummary: 當Countess Jeanne Countess of Marle and Soissons de Bar於1415年出生在法國哈帕格-亞登奧布巴-素爾-奧布時，她的父親Eduard de Bar III37歲，母親Jeanne de Bethune15歲。她於1435年7月16日與Louis de Luxembourg I, de St Pol在法國皮卡迪艾斯結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1462年5月14日在法國皮卡迪艾斯索頌斯享年47歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Countess Jeanne Countess of Marle and Soissons de Bar于1415年出生在法国哈帕格-亞登奧布巴-素爾-奧布时，她的父亲Eduard de Bar III37岁，母亲Jeanne de Bethune15岁。她于1435年7月16日与Louis de Luxembourg I, de St Pol在法国皮卡迪艾斯结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1462年5月14日在法国皮卡迪艾斯索頌斯享年47岁。

nameMeanings.name: Bar
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Moshe, Giora, Ilan, Arie, Eliahu, Hadassa, Haim, Menashe, Mordechai, Nadav, Nir, Noam.  French Dany, Francois, Henri.  German Hans, Gerhard, Willi.
Jewish (Ashkenazic; also Bär): from the Yiddish male personal name Ber (see Baer 2).
German (Bär): variant of Baer 1 ‘bear’ and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this and of its cognate Bähr (see Bahr 2).

variants.name: Baer
meanings: German (also Bär): from Middle High German ber ‘bear’, a nickname for someone thought to resemble the animal in some way, a metonymic occupational name for someone who kept a performing bear, or a topographic or habitational name referring to a house distinguished by the sign of a bear. In some cases it may derive from a personal name containing this element. Compare Bar 2.
Jewish (Ashkenazic; also Bär): from the Yiddish male personal name Ber, from Yiddish ber ‘bear’. Compare Bar 1.
Germanized form (also Bär) of Sorbian Běr: see Bar 3.

variants.name: Dubar
meanings: French (mainly Nord): habitational name, with fused preposition and definite article du ‘from the’, denoting someone from any of several places called Bar (see Bar ).

variants.name: Debar
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Laurent, Pierre.
French: habitational name, with fused preposition de ‘from’, denoting someone from any of several places called Bar (see Bar ).

nameMeanings.name: Countess
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Nottinghamshire, of Norman origin): from Middle English contas(e), Old French contesse ‘countess’, applied as a nickname for a proud, haughty woman or for a foppish man, or as an occupational name for a servant of a countess.

variants.name: Countiss
meanings: English: variant of   Countess  .

nameMeanings.name: Jeanne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Feminine form of   Jean .

variants.name: Jane
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

nameMeanings.name: Countess
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Nottinghamshire, of Norman origin): from Middle English contas(e), Old French contesse ‘countess’, applied as a nickname for a proud, haughty woman or for a foppish man, or as an occupational name for a servant of a countess.

variants.name: Countiss
meanings: English: variant of   Countess  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Oswald or any of the various other names beginning with Os-.

nameMeanings.name: Marle
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of Maria . When first introduced to England in the Middle Ages, it was Anglicized in pronunciation and respelled Mary . This French form was reintroduced into the English-speaking world as a separate name in the 19th century, and is still pronounced more or less in the French manner, although sometimes with the stress on the first syllable. It is now often used in combination with other names such as Ellie, Chloe, and Lisa.

nameMeanings.name: and
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Hebrew. See Anne .

nameMeanings.name: Soissons
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English:
variant of Sisson , with post-medieval excrescent -s.
variant of   Sessions  .

variants.name: Sisson
meanings: English:
from the Middle English female personal name Sis(se), Cisse, a pet form of Cecily (see Sisley ) + -son.
variant of Syson, a habitational name from Syston (Leicestershire), from a short form of an Old English personal name such as Sigehǣth or Sigefrith + Old English tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of any of the given names beginning with the letter D- ( compare Jay and Kay ), in particular Dorothy . It is also used as an independent name, and may in some cases be associated with the River Dee ( compare Clyde ).

en.lifeSummary: When Countess Jeanne Countess of Marle and Soissons de Bar was born in 1415, in Bar-sur-Aube, Aube, Champagne-Ardenne, France, her father, Eduard de Bar III, was 37 and her mother, Jeanne de Bethune, was 15. She married Louis de Luxembourg I, de St Pol on 16 July 1435, in Aisne, Picardie, France. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 14 May 1462, in Soissons, Aisne, Picardie, France, at the age of 47.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Countess Jeanne Countess of Marle and Soissons de Bar est née en 1415, à Bar-sur-Aube, Aube, Champagne-Ardenne, France, son père, Eduard de Bar III, avait 37 ans et sa mère, Jeanne de Bethune, avait 15 ans. Elle a épousé Louis de Luxembourg I, de St Pol le 16 juillet 1435, à Aisne, Picardie, France. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 14 mai 1462, à Soissons, Aisne, Picardie, France, à l’âge de 47 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Countess Jeanne Countess of Marle and Soissons de Bar 1415 in Bar-sur-Aube, Aube, Champagne-Ardenne, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Eduard de Bar III 37 und ihre Mutter Jeanne de Bethune 15. Sie hat am 16. Juli 1435 in Aisne, Picardie, Frankreich Louis de Luxembourg I, de St Pol geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb am 14. Mai 1462 im Alter von 47 Jahren in Soissons, Aisne, Picardie, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Countess Jeanne Countess of Marle and Soissons de Bar è nata il nel 1415 nel Bar-sur-Aube, Aube, Champagne-Ardenne, Francia, sua padre, Eduard de Bar III, aveva 37 anni e sua madre, Jeanne de Bethune, aveva 15 anni. Lei sposato Louis de Luxembourg I, de St Pol il 16 luglio 1435, nel Aisne, Picardie, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 14 maggio 1462, nel Soissons, Aisne, Picardie, Francia, all'età di 47 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Countess Jeanne Countess of Marle and Soissons de Barが1415年にフランスチャンプアグネ-アルデンアウベバー-スル-アウベで生まれた時，父Eduard de Bar IIIは37歳，母Jeanne de Bethuneは15歳であった。1435年7月16日にフランスピカーディエイスンでLouis de Luxembourg I, de St Polと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1462年5月14日にフランスピカーディエイスンソイソンスで47歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Countess Jeanne Countess of Marle and Soissons de Bar(이)가 1415년 프랑스 챔파그니-아르데니 오브 바-수-오브에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Eduard de Bar III(은)는 37세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Jeanne de Bethune(은)는 15세였다. 그녀는 1435년 7월 16일 프랑스 피카디 아이스니에서 Louis de Luxembourg I, de St Pol(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1462년 5월 14일 프랑스 피카디 아이스니 소이손스에서 47세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Countess Jeanne Countess of Marle and Soissons de Bar nasceu em 1415, em Bar-sur-Aube, Aube, Champagne-Ardenne, França, seu pai, Eduard de Bar III, tinha 37 anos e sua mãe, Jeanne de Bethune, tinha 15 anos. Ela casou-se com Louis de Luxembourg I, de St Pol em 16 de julho de 1435, em Aisne, Picardie, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 14 de maio de 1462, em Soissons, Aisne, Picardie, França, com 47 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Countess Jeanne Countess of Marle and Soissons de Bar родилась в 1415 г. в местности бар-сур-ауб, ауб, чампагне-арденн, Франция, её отцу по имени Eduard de Bar III было 37 лет, а матери, которую звали Jeanne de Bethune, 15. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Louis de Luxembourg I, de St Pol 16 июля 1435 г. в местности эисн, пикарди, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла 14 мая 1462 г. в местности соиссонс, эисн, пикарди, Франция в возрасте 47 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Countess Jeanne Countess of Marle and Soissons de Bar nació en 1415, en Bar-sur-Aube, Aube, Champagne-Ardenne, Francia, su papá, Eduard de Bar III, tenía 37 años y su mamá, Jeanne de Bethune, tenía 15 años. Se casó con Louis de Luxembourg I, de St Pol el 16 de julio de 1435, en Aisne, Picardie, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 14 de mayo de 1462, en Soissons, Aisne, Picardie, Francia, a la edad de 47 años.
